name: Make sure secrets are not passed as container environment variables
body:
### Overview

Cyscale fails this control when an active ECS task definition passes sensitive AWS credential variables such as `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, or `ECS_ENGINE_AUTH_DATA` through plain environment entries.

Secrets should be delivered through AWS Secrets Manager, SSM Parameter Store, task roles, or the ECS `secrets` block instead of visible environment variables.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Move secrets out of plain environment variables.

1. Open the affected ECS task definition.
2. Remove sensitive keys from `containerDefinitions[].environment`, especially `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, and `ECS_ENGINE_AUTH_DATA`.
3. Store the secret in AWS Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store.
4. Reference it through `containerDefinitions[].secrets` or replace static credentials with an ECS task role.
5. Register a new task definition revision and update the service to use it.

Task definition pattern:

```json
{
  "secrets": [
    {
      "name": "APP_SECRET",
      "valueFrom": "arn:aws:secretsmanager:<region>:<account-id>:secret:<secret-name>"
    }
  ]
}
```

Cyscale will pass when the active task definition no longer contains the flagged secret keys in plain environment variables.
description: Amazon ECS task definitions that pass sensitive values as plain container environment variables.
